folio.label: 119r
folio.l: And that thy smok , had leyn vp oon thy brest
ña Sire q¶ she , ©e moun seyn as ©ow lest
But Sire , a man , that waketú out of his slepe
He may nat so sodeynly , take kepe
Vp oon a thyngö , ne seyn it so ¹fitly
Til that he be a waked , verrayly
Rigút so a man , æ longe hatú blynd I be
Ne may nougút sodeynly , so wel se
First , whan his sigút´ is newe comyn a geyn
As he that hatú a day , or tweyne seyn
Til that ©oure sigút´ be satled a while
There may ful many a sigút , ©ow begyle
Betú war I preye ©ow , for by heuene kyngö
Ful many a man wenetú , to seyn a thyngö
And it is al a nother , than it semetú
He that mys conceyuetú , mys demetú
And witú that word , she lepe doun fro the tree
This Ianuarie , who is glad but he
He kissitú hire , and clippetú hire ful ofte
And oon hire wombe , he streked hire ful softe
And to his Paleys , hom he hatú hire ladde
Now goode men , I preye ©ow alle betú gladde
Thus endetú heere my tale , of Ianuarie
God blisse vs alle , and oure lady Seynt Marie
¢ Sic definit fabula Mäcatoris ,
